perturbation.original_text: Service Provider shall identify, procure, obtain, maintain and comply with all permits that may be required under applicable laws for or in connection with the performance of Services (and Non-Covered Services as actually provided by Service Provider) and that need to be procured, obtained and maintained by or in the name of Service Provider. Owner shall provide Service Provider with such assistance and cooperation as may reasonably be required in order to obtain and maintain all such Permits. Service Provider shall submit copies of all applications for, and proposed forms of, all such Permits to Owner with sufficient time to allow for Owner's review and approval.
perturbation.changed_text: Service Provider shall identify, procure, obtain, maintain and comply with all permits that may be required under applicable laws for or in connection with the performance of Services. The Service provider is not required to obtain permits in order to perform Non-Covered Services. Owner shall provide Service Provider with such assistance and cooperation as may reasonably be required in order to obtain and maintain all such Permits. Service Provider shall submit copies of all applications for, and proposed forms of, all such Permits to Owner with sufficient time to allow for Owner's review and approval.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that the Service Provider is not required to obtain permits for Non-Covered Services, while the original text implies that permits are required for both Services and Non-Covered Services if the Service Provider actually provides them. The contradiction creates uncertainty regarding the obligation of the Service Provider to obtain permits for Non-Covered Services, since section 1.4 states that "The Services shall not include, and Service Provider shall not be responsible for, any operations, maintenance, repair, or other services beyond the Services set forth in Exhibit C (such non-covered services referred to hereinafter as 'Non-Covered Services')". In addition the original text states that Service Provider is responsible to obtain permits for Non-Covered Services, however the modified text is stating that the Service Provider is not responsible to obtain permits.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Service Provider shall identify, procure, obtain, maintain and comply with all permits that may be required under applicable laws for or in connection with the performance of Services. The Service provider is not required to obtain permits in order to perform Non-Covered Services. Owner shall provide Service Provider with such assistance and cooperation as may reasonably be required in order to obtain and maintain all such Permits. Service Provider shall submit copies of all applications for, and proposed forms of, all such Permits to Owner with sufficient time to allow for Owner's review and approval.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text and by extension the contradicted text. The original text requires the Service Provider to obtain permits for Non-Covered Services if they are actually provided, while the changed text explicitly states that the Service Provider is *not* required to obtain permits for Non-Covered Services. This creates a direct conflict, resulting in a strong in-text contradiction. The contradiction is further emphasized by the fact that section 1.4 states that 'The Services shall not include, and Service Provider shall not be responsible for, any operations, maintenance, repair, or other services beyond the Services set forth in Exhibit C (such non-covered services referred to hereinafter as 'Non-Covered Services')', which implies that Non-Covered Services exist and that the permit requirements for them are now unclear. Because the Changed Text directly conflicts with the Contradicted Text a 'YES' is assigned.

perturbation.original_text: Within five (5) days after the termination or expiration of this Agreement, and upon Owner's payment in full of the amounts due Service Provider under the Agreement, Service Provider shall deliver to Owner all of the SEF O&M Reports, SEF books, records and property in its possession or under its control, and all materials, supplies, consumables, manuals and any other items furnished to Service Provider by Owner.
perturbation.changed_text: Within five (5) days after the termination or expiration of this Agreement, Service Provider shall deliver to Owner all of the SEF O&M Reports, SEF books, records and property in its possession or under its control, and all materials, supplies, consumables, manuals and any other items furnished to Service Provider by Owner. Any payments owed to Service Provider are forfeited upon termination.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the condition for delivering the SEF O&M Reports and other materials. The original text specifies that the Service Provider must deliver these items 'upon Owner's payment in full of the amounts due'. However, the modified text states that 'Any payments owed to Service Provider are forfeited upon termination', effectively eliminating the condition of payment. This creates an in-text contradiction because it is unclear whether the Service Provider is obligated to deliver the materials regardless of outstanding payments, creating uncertainty of both parties responsibilites. It is creating an in-text contradiction between obligations after termination of contracts.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Within five (5) days after the termination or expiration of this Agreement, Service Provider shall deliver to Owner all of the SEF O&M Reports, SEF books, records and property in its possession or under its control, and all materials, supplies, consumables, manuals and any other items furnished to Service Provider by Owner. Any payments owed to Service Provider are forfeited upon termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct in-text contradiction. The original text makes the delivery of SEF O&M Reports contingent upon 'Owner's payment in full of the amounts due Service Provider'. The changed text then states 'Any payments owed to Service Provider are forfeited upon termination'. This eliminates the condition of payment, thus creating a contradiction since the Service Provider is now obligated to deliver the items regardless of whether they are paid. This falls under the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type because the condition for delivering the reports is placed in the same location and contradicts the payment clause.

perturbation.original_text: Owner shall furnish reasonable access to the Premises in order to allow Service Provider to perform the Services. Service Provider's access to the Premises (including its agents, employees, and representatives) shall be subject to User's rules and regulations, security policies and guidelines and access control systems and procedures (as provided by Owner to Service Provider as of the Effective Date). Owner shall coordinate and provide for User's, or User's agent's, supervision of Service Provider, as may be required by User, in a manner that shall not disrupt Service Provider's performance of the Services.
perturbation.changed_text: Owner shall furnish reasonable access to the Premises in order to allow Service Provider to perform the Services. Service Provider's access to the Premises (including its agents, employees, and representatives) shall be unrestricted and not subject to any rules or regulations. Owner shall coordinate and provide for User's, or User's agent's, supervision of Service Provider, as may be required by User, in a manner that shall not disrupt Service Provider's performance of the Services.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces a contradiction by removing the User's rules and regulations, security policies, guidelines, and access control systems as conditions for the Service Provider's access to the Premises. The original text subjects the Service Provider's access to these conditions, while the revised text states that access shall be 'unrestricted and not subject to any rules or regulations.' This contradiction could lead to disputes regarding whether the Service Provider must comply with security protocols or other access limitations imposed by the User, affecting performance of service and contract
perturbation.contradicted_text: Owner shall furnish reasonable access to the Premises in order to allow Service Provider to perform the Services. Service Provider's access to the Premises (including its agents, employees, and representatives) shall be unrestricted and not subject to any rules or regulations. Owner shall coordinate and provide for User's, or User's agent's, supervision of Service Provider, as may be required by User, in a manner that shall not disrupt Service Provider's performance of the Services.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Service Provider's access to the premises shall be 'unrestricted and not subject to any rules or regulations', while the original text (and, initially, the contradicted text) stated that access was 'subject to User's rules and regulations, security policies and guidelines and access control systems and procedures'. This creates a direct contradiction regarding whether the Service Provider must comply with those rules, affecting the performance of the service and contract.
